question: What specific events could re-rate or further de-rate NVO in the next 6 months?

findings.claim: Oral Wegovy (semaglutide pill) was FDA-approved on December 22, 2025, making it the first oral GLP-1 for obesity in the US. US launch began early January 2026.
findings.data: FDA approved once-daily Wegovy pill based on OASIS 4 Phase 3 trial (307 adults, 64 weeks). Oral semaglutide 25mg achieved 16.6% mean weight loss at 64 weeks vs 2.7% for placebo. One-third of adherent participants achieved >=20% weight loss. Weight loss comparable to injectable Wegovy 2.4mg.

findings.claim: Oral Wegovy uptake has been exceptionally strong — 170,000+ patients in approximately 4 weeks, more than double prior branded anti-obesity drug launches.
findings.data: CEO Doustdar at Q4 2025 earnings (Feb 4, 2026): 'Put plainly, there are now more than 170,000 people on the Wegovy pill in about four weeks. This uptake is more than double the previous branded anti-obesity drugs launched in the U.S.'

findings.claim: Oral Wegovy starting price set at $149/month for self-pay, far below injectable pricing, which pressures ASP but drives volume.
findings.data: Starting dose of 1.5mg available at $149/month through pharmacies and telehealth providers. Hims & Hers opened waitlist for $149 branded Wegovy and Ozempic via Novo Nordisk deal (announced March 18, 2026).

findings.claim: CATALYST: Oral Wegovy prescription volume trend will be a key metric at Q1 2026 earnings (May 6, 2026). Strong ramp = potential re-rating catalyst.
findings.data: 170,000 patients in ~4 weeks is a strong signal, but sustainability and conversion to higher doses is uncertain. Next data point: May 6 earnings. Direction: RE-RATE (if volume exceeds expectations) or DE-RATE (if growth plateaus or cannibalization of injectable is severe).
